# Preface {.unnumbered}

<!-- <https://quarto.org/docs/books>. -->

These notes are prepared for the MECH230 Dynamics course at the American University of Beirut (AUB), Lebanon. The content is adapted primarily from [O’Reilly’s Dynamics](https://link.springer.com/content/pdf/10.1007/978-3-030-11745-0.pdf) Primer, with selected examples and exercises drawn from MKB's and Hibbeler's classic textbooks.

These notes begin with simpler examples to build a strong foundation, enabling students to confidently tackle more complex problems. They are not comprehensive; students should refer to the course textbooks as needed.

I am grateful for the perspectives on dynamics of Professor Noel Perkins (University of Michigan–Ann Arbor), Professor James Casey (UC Berkeley), and Dr. Evan Hemingway, which I gained while TAing Berkeley’s ME104. I am also thankful to Professor Oliver O'Reilly for his excellent book on dynamics, which has been an invaluable resource for both me and my students, as well as for his many helpful discussions and advice on the subject.

If you find any mistakes in these notes, please let me know by filling out [this](https://forms.gle/McKpRhMq4JejnG8V9) form.
